commit.message:
fix(devcontainer): generate project-specific machine-id

Generate /etc/machine-id from project folder name hash to maintain
consistent container identity across rebuilds while ensuring different
projects have different IDs.

Format: md5sum of "devcontainer-${localWorkspaceFolderBasename}"

commit.message:
fix(devcontainer): create .claude.json to prevent auth re-confirmation

Add creation of ~/.claude/.claude.json in postCreateCommand.
Without this file, Claude Code treats the environment as a new
installation and requests re-authentication on every container rebuild.

Refs #8

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
fix: player start on road, cleaner road texture, NPC road constraint

- Fix player starting position to be on actual road intersection
- Improve canPlayerEnter with margin check (half-tile tolerance)
- Allow entry near POIs (2 tiles) and landmarks (3 tiles)
- NPC cars destroyed when pushed off-road by collisions
- Cleaner road texture: removed outer borders, kept only inner
  yellow dashed center lines for lane separation
- Road surface is clean dark asphalt without distracting borders

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement P3.5: INT (0xCD) and IRET (0xCF) + fix UM FSM load pipeline bugs

INT real-mode: PUSH_FLAGS, CLI, PUSH CS, PUSH EIP, INT_ENTER (IVT read).
IRET real-mode: POP EIP, POP CS, POP_FLAGS, INT_EXIT (seg_cache + redirect).

Fix two UM FSM bugs that caused microcode load data corruption:
- CDB1 tag filtering: UM_LOAD_WAIT now checks cdb1_tag == macro_rob_tag
  to reject non-microcode load completions (uc_cdb1_match wire).
- Load pipeline collision: gate uc_mem_ld_fire on !uc_load_pipe_busy
  (driven by ld_in_flight) to prevent microcode loads from firing while
  a non-microcode load occupies the LSQ ld_state pipeline.
- Load result forwarding: uc_prev_load_result register passes previous
  micro-op load data to next micro-op (rename_com_map is stale mid-seq).

Tests 26-30 in tb_ooo_core_ucode_mem: INT basic, CS:EIP redirect, stack
frame data, IRET basic, INT+IRET roundtrip. 106 checks, 11/11 ctest.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Woodpecker agent auth: use manually-managed agent token secret

Woodpecker 3.x requires individual agent tokens (not shared secrets).
Point agent to woodpecker-agent-secret (manually created with the
server-registered agent token) instead of the Helm-generated default.

commit.message:
fix: update to March 2026 data, Middle East map center, flight-first card layout

Worker.js — KNOWN_ONGOING updated for March 2026 reality:
- TLV: CRITICAL→HIGH — many airlines resumed post-Jan 2025 Gaza ceasefire
- BEY: CRITICAL→HIGH — airlines returning after Nov 2024 Lebanon ceasefire
- SAA: CRITICAL (unchanged) — still closed, zero commercial flights
- Gaza (GZA/null): NEW entry — airspace = combat zone, airport destroyed
- BGW: HIGH (updated language — describes what IS currently operating)
- DAM: CRITICAL→HIGH — post-Assad transition, recovery phase
- Russia/UUWW: NEW entry — airspace closed to 150+ Western carriers since 2022
- IKA: HIGH (updated — current sanctions + closure risk)
- ADE: HIGH (updated — Houthi campaign current status)
- New field: current_flights_impacted — names specific airlines/routes NOW
- AI prompt: asks for CURRENT disruptions, flight-route level, specific airlines

index.html:
- Map center: [20,0] (Atlantic) → [28,42] (Middle East), zoom 2→3
- Light mode adaptive tiles: CartoDB light_all when light-mode active
- Card redesign: flight-first — shows current_flights_impacted prominently
  above the AI summary with blue accent border (was buried in small meta row)
- airport-header-right: IATA + name + tags in column layout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix double sign-in failure with cooldown timer

Supabase rate-limits OTP emails to once per 60 seconds. Previously a
second attempt showed a cryptic "Failed. Try again." Now the button
disables for 60s after sending, shows a countdown, and the error
message says "Link already sent — check your email" instead of failing.

commit.message:
feat(mcp): add Streamable HTTP transport for Claude.ai MCP connector

Implement MCP 2025-03-26 Streamable HTTP transport alongside existing
legacy SSE transport. Claude.ai uses POST /sse with JSON-RPC bodies
instead of the legacy GET /sse + separate POST /message flow.

Changes:
- POST /sse: authenticates Bearer token, creates/reuses DO session via
  Mcp-Session-Id header, forwards JSON-RPC to DO, returns JSON directly
- DELETE /sse: returns 405 (session termination not supported)
- CORS: allow Mcp-Session-Id in requests, expose it in responses
- session.ts: extract processRpcMessage() shared by both transports,
  add /streamable DO route for direct JSON-RPC request/response
- protocol.ts: fix sseEvent() double-quoting strings (caused /%22 URLs)
- Protocol negotiation: fall back to 2025-03-26 for unknown versions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: cap http_get() response body at 10 MiB (issue #17) (#18)

* fix: quality audit — architecture + parity fixes

Multi-stage audit findings addressed:

Architecture (MAJOR):
- Add ProjectDirs struct to centralize all .claude path definitions
  (eliminates 20+ scattered .join('.claude') chains)
- Replace direct std::env::current_dir() in business logic with
  ProjectDirs::from_cwd() for consistency and testability
- Add RAII TempScript guard in PythonBridge (cleanup even on panic)

Security (MAJOR):
- Block shell expansion patterns ($(…), backticks, $VAR, ${VAR})
  in CWD deletion/rename protection — prevents bypass via command
  substitution

Parity (CRITICAL):
- Power steering: implement Python bridge delegation instead of stub
- Session start version check: delegate to Python bridge instead of
  returning None
- Session start hook migration: actually remove global hooks via
  Python bridge instead of just detecting them
- Preference parsing: support both table format and ### header format
  for Python parity
- Lock counter file extension: .json → .txt for cross-version compat

Quality (MINOR):
- Add re-exports to amplihack-hooks lib.rs
- Document all intentional error swallowing with comments
- Remove unsafe env::set_var from tests (use pre-seeded counters
  and default-based tests instead)
- Remove unused imports (Arc, Barrier, Path)

All 91 tests pass. Clippy clean. Fmt clean.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add stop reflection + launcher detection (f12, f13)

- Stop reflection: delegates to Python SDK bridge for post-session
  analysis. Reads transcript JSONL, calls run_claude_reflection(),
  saves FEEDBACK_SUMMARY.md, blocks with findings. Semaphore prevents
  re-presentation. Opt-in via AMPLIHACK_ENABLE_REFLECTION env var.

- Launcher detection: detects Claude Code / Copilot / Amplifier via
  env vars. Copilot injects context into AGENTS.md via Python bridge.
  Claude Code uses default .claude/ pull model. Security checks (CWD,
  branch, --no-verify) are independent of detected launcher.

All 95 tests pass. Clippy clean. Fmt clean.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove silent ImportError fallbacks from all bridge scripts

All 7 Python bridge scripts embedded in Rust hooks previously had
`except ImportError: return empty` patterns. These are now removed —
imports are top-level so ImportError crashes with non-zero exit.

Affected bridges:
- session_start.rs: MEMORY_CONTEXT_BRIDGE, VERSION_CHECK_BRIDGE
- session_stop.rs: MEMORY_STORE_BRIDGE
- user_prompt.rs: MEMORY_INJECT_BRIDGE
- launcher.rs: COPILOT_CONTEXT_BRIDGE
- power_steering.rs: POWER_STEERING_BRIDGE
- reflection.rs: REFLECTION_BRIDGE

Related: rysweet/amplihack#2982

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: R2 audit — reflection race condition, dead code, error handling

- reflection.rs: Replace check-then-set semaphore with atomic
  OpenOptions::create_new() to prevent TOCTOU race
- reflection.rs: Log fs::write() errors instead of silent let _ =
- launcher.rs: Change inject_context() from Option<Value> to () — it
  always returned None, creating dead code in caller
- pre_tool_use/mod.rs: Remove dead if-let-Some branch for inject_context
- paths.rs: Add global_settings() method to ProjectDirs
- paths.rs: Log CWD fallback in from_cwd() instead of silent fallback
- session_start.rs: Use ProjectDirs::global_settings() instead of HOME

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: critical parity gaps — uncommitted work, reflection, git timeout

Parity fixes from R2 Stage 3 recheck:

- session_stop.rs: Add uncommitted work detection (git status check
  with staged/unstaged/untracked categorization and recovery hints)
- reflection.rs: Per-session semaphore (.reflection_presented_{id}),
  timestamped output files for history, semaphore written AFTER success
  (fixes code review finding: stale semaphore on bridge failure)
- git.rs: Add 5-second timeout via try_wait poll loop (matches Python)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- user_prompt.rs: Add /dev invocation detection for workflow enforcement

- post_tool_use.rs: Tool-specific validation for Write/Edit/MultiEdit

- post_tool_use.rs: 5 new unit tests for categorization + validation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Ubuntu <azureuser@devy.yb0a3bvkdghunmsjr4s3fnfhra.phxx.internal.cloudapp.net>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Phase 2 — CLI + launcher crate with clap, ManagedChild, signals, nesting detection

Phase 2 implementation:

2a — CLI command parsing with clap derive:
- Full CLI struct with all subcommands (Install, Uninstall, Launch,
  Claude, Copilot, Codex, Amplifier, Plugin, Memory, Recipe, Mode, Version)
- Sub-enums for Plugin (install/uninstall/link/verify),
  Memory (tree/export/import/clean), Recipe (run/list/validate/show),
  Mode (detect/to-plugin/to-local)
- Python delegation via subprocess for memory, recipe, plugin, mode commands
- Command dispatch module routing parsed args to handlers

2b — Launcher + process management:
- ManagedChild with bounded Drop (SIGTERM → 3s wait → SIGKILL)
- Signal handling via signal-hook (SIGINT, SIGTERM, SIGHUP → AtomicBool)
- Child processes in own process group via setpgid (prevents double-SIGINT)
- Binary finder with PATH search, env override, version detection
- Type-safe EnvBuilder with set-based PATH deduplication
- Settings manager using AtomicJsonFile for atomic settings.json CRUD

2c — Nesting detection:
- Primary: AMPLIHACK_SESSION_ID env var check
- Secondary: Session file (PID + timestamp) in ~/.claude/runtime/
- Stale detection: dead PID + >1h old = stale (ignored)
- Session claim/release for lifecycle management

Also updates bins/amplihack to use the new CLI crate and adds
workspace deps (clap, signal-hook, libc).

35 new tests across all modules.

Co-authored-by: Ubuntu <azureuser@devy.yb0a3bvkdghunmsjr4s3fnfhra.phxx.internal.cloudapp.net>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: session_count filename parity with Python

Python writes to 'session_count', Rust was writing to 'session_count.json'.
This broke cross-engine power steering counter visibility.

Co-authored-by: Ubuntu <azureuser@devy.yb0a3bvkdghunmsjr4s3fnfhra.phxx.internal.cloudapp.net>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: land native CLI parity tiers (#10)

* feat: Phase 3 parity tier1 — CLI commands, bridge scripts, integration tests

- Implement full CLI command suite: install, launch, plugin, memory, recipe,
  mode with all subcommands (5,013 lines added across 11 files)
- Add command_error.rs (exit code handling) and test_support.rs (test utils)
- Populate bridge/ with 4 Python subprocess bridge scripts covering all
  SDK-dependent hook functions with machine-readable JSON IPC contracts:
  - bridge/reflection.py       → StopHook._run_reflection_sync()
  - bridge/session_start.py    → SessionStartHook.process()
  - bridge/session_stop.py     → session_stop.main()
  - bridge/user_prompt_submit.py → UserPromptSubmitHook.process()
- Add bridge/test_bridges.py with 22 unit tests (all passing)
- Add tests/integration/cli_launch_test.rs (9 integration tests)
- Add tests/integration/hook_dispatch_test.rs (10 integration tests)
- Wire integration tests to amplihack and amplihack-hooks-bin crates
- Add parity test scenarios (5 YAML files) and validate_cli_parity.py
- Fix clippy warning: print!() with newline → println!() in memory.rs
- All tests pass: cargo test --workspace, clippy -- -D warnings, fmt --check

commit.message:
feat: add LoRA for parameter-efficient fine-tuning (#16)

* feat: add LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) for efficient fine-tuning

Implement LoRALinear module wrapping nn.Linear with low-rank A/B
matrices (Hu et al. 2021). B is zero-initialized so initial output
matches the base model. Includes apply_lora() for targeting attention
and/or FFN layers, merge_lora() for inference, and lora_parameters()
for saving adapter weights. 15 tests covering shapes, initialization,
targeting, forward passes, trainable count, and merge correctness.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: freeze all non-LoRA params after apply_lora

Freeze entire model then selectively unfreeze lora_A/lora_B keys
so only LoRA matrices are trainable. Previously embedding and
other non-targeted layers remained trainable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Restructure into 7 chapters with collapsible support data

Replace 10 tabs + Anexos dump with 7 focused chapters:
1. Resumen Ejecutivo (4 sections)
2. Perfil de Mercado (7 primary + 6 soporte)
3. Demanda (4 primary + 14 soporte)
4. Ecosistema Competitivo (6 primary + 6 soporte)
5. Tendencias (3 primary + 3 soporte)
6. Comprador (6 sections)
7. Estrategia (9 primary + 7 soporte)
8. Panorama Mexico (9 primary + 10 soporte, national-only)

Key changes:
- Eliminate Anexos category — all 32 sections redistributed logically
- Merge Precios into Competencia (prices need competitive context)
- Merge McKinsey into Resumen + Estrategia
- Collapsible "Datos de soporte" sub-groups per chapter
- Panorama tab hidden in city view (national-only data)
- TOC sidebar shows soporte items dimmed with auto-expand on click
- Tab counts show "primary+soporte" format

Add 5-mode 3D scene generator web UI

- Flask web server (port 5000) with 5 tabbed generation modes:
  1. Text Prompt → 3D (WorldGen + FLUX.1)
  2. Image Upload → 3D (WorldGen + FLUX.1-Fill)
  3. Multi-Photo → 3D (AnySplat, SIGGRAPH Asia 2025)
  4. Multi-Photo → 3D (HunyuanWorld-Mirror, Tencent SOTA)
  5. Video → 3D (HunyuanWorld-Mirror with auto frame extraction)
- Interactive 3D Gaussian Splat viewer via Viser (port 8080)
- Drag-and-drop uploads, live previews, progress tracking
- Smart process management to prevent GPU OOM
- Adjustable FPS for video mode, frame capping for long videos

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add service worker, offline support, and Hetzner deploy config (Phase 7)

Task 7.1 — Service worker with cache-first for static assets, network-first
for API, offline fallback page, versioned caches, skip-waiting on activate.
Registration only in production. 4 SW tests.

Tasks 7.3+7.4 — Hetzner deployment: pm2 cluster config, nginx with SSL
(Let's Encrypt), gzip, rate limiting, security headers. Production server
entry point with @hono/node-server. Setup and deploy shell scripts.

308 tests total (110 Rust + 9 types + 215 renderer + 84 web).

commit.message:
Add climate anomaly methods, results summary, and trend table (#119)

- Methods: document ERA5-Land source, anomaly calculation, trend analysis
- Results: plain-language narrative — watershed ~2°C warmer than mid-20th
  century, precipitation unchanged, summer soils drying ~5% despite stable
  rainfall due to increased evapotranspiration
- Appendix: trend table with Total Change column (slope x years) so readers
  see cumulative impact, plain-language summary of three key patterns,
  spatial anomaly maps from BC Climate Anomaly app
- Data: CSV time series and trend summaries extracted programmatically
  from ERA5-Land NetCDF (NewGraphEnvironment/bc_climate_anomaly#2)
- Bump version to 0.1.7

Fixes #118
Relates to #20
Relates to NewGraphEnvironment/sred-2025-2026#4

commit.message:
Add 40 integration tests for generation flow wiring across all 4 flows

The A11y Analyze bug (missing authToken) exposed a gap: no tests exercised
the path from button click to provider creation. This adds integration tests
for useAIGenerate hook, AccessibilityResult, TestDataTab, IdeasTab, CodeTab,
and App cross-tab flow — verifying correct provider config, auth token
wiring, streaming, error handling, and signOut/refreshUsage behavior.

259 → 299 tests across 28 → 30 files.
